title: Antiderivatives, Riemann Sums, and The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus

frontMatter.title: Antiderivatives, Riemann Sums, and The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
frontMatter.course: MA 103 Calculus of Single Variable and Linear Algebra
frontMatter.summary: Covers the definition of antiderivatives, indefinite integrals, Riemann sums for area approximation, and the two parts of the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus.
table_of_contents: Initial Value Problems and Differential Equations
Types of Riemann Sums in Sigma Notation
Net Area and the Definite Integral
The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus Part I
The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus Part II

key_formulas.name: Antiderivative Definition
key_formulas.note: A function $F$ is an antiderivative of $f$ if its derivative is $f$.

key_formulas.name: General Antiderivative
key_formulas.note: The family of all antiderivatives of $f(x)$ includes an arbitrary constant $C$.

key_formulas.name: Power Rule for Antidifferentiation
key_formulas.note: The integral of $x$ raised to a power.

key_formulas.name: Indefinite Integral
key_formulas.note: The notation for the set of all antiderivatives of $f(x)$.

key_formulas.name: Riemann Sum (General)
key_formulas.note: Approximation of the area under a curve using $n$ rectangles.

key_formulas.name: Left Riemann Sum
key_formulas.note: Sample point is the left endpoint of the subinterval.

key_formulas.name: Right Riemann Sum
key_formulas.note: Sample point is the right endpoint of the subinterval.

key_formulas.name: Midpoint Riemann Sum
key_formulas.note: Sample point is the midpoint of the subinterval.

key_formulas.name: Definite Integral
key_formulas.note: The exact net area under $f(x)$ from $a$ to $b$.

key_formulas.name: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus Part I (Evaluation)
key_formulas.note: Connects definite integrals with antiderivatives, where $F'(x) = f(x)$.

key_formulas.name: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus Part II (Differentiation)
key_formulas.note: Shows that differentiation and integration are inverse operations.
